name: Equity Research Presentation Structure (10 Slides)
description: Generates a full 10-slide equity research presentation structure covering company snapshot, investment thesis, business model, industry overview, competitive positioning, financials, segment analysis, management commentary, risks/valuation, and bull/bear/verdict.
prompt_text:
Make a Equity Research Presentation Structure (10 Slides). Do not give me the ppt, just the slides.

Company Name: [INSERT COMPANY NAME]

## Slide 1 — Company Snapshot
- Full company name
- NSE/BSE ticker
- Market cap
- Headquarters
- CEO / MD
- Business description
- Revenue mix by geography and segment
- Key stats: revenue, EBITDA margin / EBIT margin, PAT, ROE, employee count

## Slide 2 — Investment Thesis
- 5–7 key reasons why the company is interesting
- Key growth drivers
- Why the company can outperform
- Major structural tailwinds
- Major concerns or headwinds

## Slide 3 — Business Model and Revenue Mix
- Core business segments
- Product / service mix
- Geography mix
- Customer mix
- Vertical mix
- Major industries served
- Distribution network / manufacturing footprint / client concentration trends
- Domestic vs export mix if relevant

## Slide 4 — Industry Overview
- Industry size
- Industry growth outlook
- Key trends shaping the sector
- Competitive landscape
- Regulatory environment if relevant
- Where the company is positioned within the industry

## Slide 5 — Competitive Positioning
| Company | Revenue | EBITDA Margin / EBIT Margin | Growth | Key Strength |
|----------|----------|-----------------------------|--------|---------------|
| Company Name | | | | |
| Peer 1 | | | | |
| Peer 2 | | | | |
| Peer 3 | | | | |
| Peer 4 | | | | |
- Company strengths
- Weaknesses
- Market share trends
- Position across key segments / geographies / products

## Slide 6 — Financial Performance
### Revenue Trend
| Metric | FY22 | FY23 | FY24 | FY25 |
|---------|------|------|------|------|
| Revenue | | | | |
| Revenue Growth % | | | | |
| EBITDA / EBIT | | | | |
| EBITDA Margin % / EBIT Margin % | | | | |
| PAT | | | | |
| PAT Margin % | | | | |
| EPS | | | | |
| ROE | | | | |
| ROCE | | | | |

### Balance Sheet Highlights
- Cash
- Debt
- Net Cash / Net Debt
- Net Worth

### Cash Flow Highlights
- CFO
- FCF
- Capex
- Dividend

## Slide 7 — Segment and Geography Analysis
- Segment-wise revenue mix
- Geography-wise growth
- Margin profile by segment if available
- Which segments are driving growth
- Which segments are slowing
- Domestic vs export mix
- Key customer categories

## Slide 8 — Management Commentary and Key Growth Drivers
- Demand outlook
- Margin guidance
- Pricing environment
- Capex plans
- Hiring / employee trends if relevant
- Order book / deal wins
- New product launches
- Capacity expansion
- Export outlook
- Industry tailwinds
- Structural growth drivers

## Slide 9 — Risks and Valuation
### Risks
- Demand slowdown
- Pricing pressure
- Margin pressure
- Raw material cost inflation
- Currency volatility
- Regulatory risks
- Competition
- Customer concentration
- Technology disruption
- Capital allocation risks

### Valuation
| Metric | Current | Historical Average |
|---------|---------|--------------------|
| P/E | | |
| EV/EBITDA | | |
| Price to Book | | |
| Dividend Yield | | |
- Whether the stock trades at a premium or discount
- Why the valuation is justified or not

## Slide 10 — Bull Case, Bear Case, and Quick Verdict
### Bull Case
- Strong growth
- Margin expansion
- Better demand
- New product launches / deal wins / capacity utilisation improvement

### Bear Case
- Weak growth
- Margin pressure
- Industry slowdown
- Higher competition

### Quick Verdict
- 5 bullet point summary
- Biggest strength
- Biggest weakness
- What investors should track
- Most important monitorable over the next 12 months

name: Dark Luxury PPT — Abstract 3D Style
description: Generates a premium dark-themed equity research slide deck with abstract 3D vantablack aesthetics, gold accents, and glass data callouts. Designed for maximum visual impact.
pro_tip: Upload a pre-researched Markdown file or concall notes first. The more structured your input, the richer the visual output.
prompt_text:
Create a dark, premium equity research slide deck with a moody vantablack background (#0A0A0A). Use 3D isometric renders as hero visuals on each slide — fractal monoliths with recursive geometric patterns, architectural buildings, geometric metallic structures, mechanical models, and topographic maps rendered as neural liquid gold structures that flow like molten circuitry, vantablack glass surfaces with sharp reflective edges, crystalline data-lattice formations, and hovering obsidian polyhedra with internal golden light bleeding through fractured seams. All 3D elements rendered in liquid gold, matte black glass, and brushed dark platinum textures with subtle subsurface scattering.

Connect the 3D hero visuals to data callouts using ultra-thin pulsing gold micro-filaments — hair-thin golden lines with tiny luminous nodes at each connection point, as if data is being transmitted through the structure.

Place key financial data points (revenue, margins, PAT, market cap, growth rates) in semi-transparent frosted vantablack glass callout boxes with 1px liquid gold borders and subtle inner glow. Each callout has: small uppercase label in muted platinum (#8A8A8A), large bold stat in gold (#c9a96e) or white, and one-line descriptor below.

Use a serif font for slide titles and a clean sans-serif for data labels. Color palette strictly: vantablack (#0A0A0A) background, liquid gold (#c9a96e) accents, dark platinum (#2A2A2A) card surfaces, off-white (#e8e0d0) text. No other colors.

Style should feel like a luxury investment research report designed inside a quantum computing lab. Each slide should have one dominant 3D visual with 3–6 data callout boxes arranged around it, connected via gold micro-filaments. No stock photos. No flat design. No gradients. No bright colors. Futuristic, minimal, institutional, dark aesthetic throughout. Every pixel should feel like it costs money.

name: Dark Luxury PPT — Industry-Specific Visuals
description: Generates a dark institutional slide deck where hero visuals are tailored to the company's actual industry — IT, banking, pharma, defence, industrials, etc. Delivers a Bloomberg/BlackRock aesthetic.
prompt_text:
Create a premium equity research slide deck with a dark institutional background using deep charcoal black (#0F1113) and graphite grey (#1A1D21). The visual style should feel like a Swiss private bank research report designed by a computational architect.

All hero visuals must be directly related to the company's industry, products, business model, customers, and value chain — not generic abstract shapes.

For example:
- IT services: futuristic server farms, cloud infrastructure, AI data centers, cyber-security networks, coding terminals
- Banking / NBFCs: premium vaults, digital payment rails, lending networks, wealth management interfaces
- Pharma: molecular structures, injectable vials, production plants, clean rooms, research labs
- Defence: radars, missiles, drones, command centers, naval systems, aerospace engineering
- Industrials: factories, assembly lines, capital goods, warehouses, precision engineering machinery
- Consumer goods: premium packaging, retail shelves, distribution networks, branded products
- Chemicals: specialty chemical reactors, process plants, molecular chains, industrial pipelines
- Power / Renewables: solar farms, transmission lines, substations, smart grids, wind turbines

The 3D hero visuals should still be premium and futuristic, but grounded in the company's actual industry. Use isometric or cinematic 3D renders with brushed titanium, smoked glass, obsidian black, dark chrome, graphite metal, muted gold, and translucent crystal textures. Include subtle reflections, soft volumetric lighting, ambient occlusion shadows, and faint glowing highlights.

Connect hero visuals to data callouts using ultra-fine metallic micro-filaments — hair-thin silver-grey lines with tiny luminous endpoints.

Place key financial data points in premium smoked-glass callout boxes with 1px muted gold (#B8A97E) borders.

Color palette strictly: deep charcoal black (#0F1113) background, graphite grey (#1A1D21) surfaces, muted gold (#B8A97E) accents, titanium grey (#7A7A72) for labels, off-white (#F2F2F0) for primary text.

Every slide should contain: one dominant industry-related 3D visual, 3–6 precise data callout boxes, abundant whitespace, premium institutional layout.

name: White Institutional PPT — Swiss Private Bank Style
description: Generates a clean ivory-white slide deck with industry-specific 3D visuals, frosted glass callouts, and platinum micro-filaments. Feels like a Goldman Sachs or McKinsey presentation.
prompt_text:
Create a premium equity research slide deck with a clean ivory-white background (#F5F3EF). The visual style should feel like a Swiss private bank research report designed by a computational architect.

All hero visuals must be directly related to the company's industry, products, business model, customers, and value chain — not generic abstract shapes.

The 3D hero visuals should be premium and futuristic, but grounded in the company's actual industry. Use isometric or cinematic 3D renders with polished titanium, frosted quartz, pale gold, and translucent glass textures. Include subtle caustic light reflections, soft ambient occlusion shadows, and ultra-clean lighting.

Connect hero visuals to data callouts using ultra-fine platinum micro-filaments — hair-thin silver-grey lines with tiny luminous endpoints.

Place key financial data points inside clean frosted glass callout boxes with 1px pale gold (#B8A97E) borders, minimal shadow, small uppercase label in warm grey (#7A7A72), large bold stat in deep charcoal (#1E1E1E) or muted gold (#B8A97E), and one-line descriptor below.

Color palette strictly: ivory white (#F5F3EF) background, pale gold (#B8A97E) accents, titanium grey (#4A4A4A) for headers, warm grey (#7A7A72) for labels, deep charcoal (#1E1E1E) for primary text.

Every slide: one dominant industry-related 3D visual, 3–6 data callout boxes, abundant whitespace, premium institutional layout.

The deck should feel like a premium institutional research presentation handed to HNIs, PMS clients, or fund managers in a private meeting room.

name: Dark Institutional Infographic — Bloomberg-Style Factsheet
description: Creates a premium one-page company infographic in a dark hedge fund / Bloomberg Terminal aesthetic. Condenses the entire investment story into a single visual page with 6–8 data blocks.
prompt_text:
Create a premium one-page infographic for an equity research report with a dark institutional background using deep charcoal black (#0F1113) and graphite grey (#1A1D21). The layout should feel like a cross between a Bloomberg terminal tear sheet, a hedge fund factsheet, and a premium consulting infographic.

The infographic should summarize the company in one page using 6–8 visual blocks:
1. Company Snapshot
2. Revenue / EBITDA / PAT / Market Cap
3. Business Segments
4. Geography Mix
5. Key Growth Drivers
6. Competitive Positioning
7. Risks
8. Valuation Snapshot
9. Quick Verdict

Visual style: premium industry-specific 3D renders, no generic abstract objects. Use subtle isometric or cinematic visuals related to the company's actual business.

Color palette: Background (#0F1113), Surface cards (#1A1D21), Accent muted gold (#B8A97E), Labels titanium grey (#A5A29B), Main text off-white (#F2F2F0), Secondary lines dark chrome/silver-grey.

Design requirements:
- Smoked-glass statistic cards with thin muted gold borders
- Subtle arrows, maps, pie charts, stacked bars, and mini trend charts
- Metallic micro-filament connectors between sections
- 3–5 key metrics in large font (Revenue, EBITDA Margin, PAT, ROE/ROCE, Market Cap)
- Premium "bull case vs bear case" section
- Small peer comparison chart
- Valuation box showing P/E, EV/EBITDA, and Price to Book

The final output should feel like a luxury hedge fund factsheet or premium institutional investor dashboard.

name: White Institutional Infographic — Swiss Private Bank Style
description: Creates a clean, premium one-page company infographic in a Swiss private bank / cotton paper aesthetic. Perfect for client-facing investor materials.
prompt_text:
Create a premium one-page infographic for an equity research report with a clean ivory-white background (#F5F3EF). The layout should feel like a cross between a Swiss private bank tear sheet, an institutional investor factsheet, and a premium consulting infographic.

The infographic should summarize the company in one page using 6–8 visual blocks:
1. Company Snapshot
2. Revenue / EBITDA / PAT / Market Cap
3. Business Segments
4. Geography Mix
5. Key Growth Drivers
6. Competitive Positioning
7. Risks
8. Valuation Snapshot
9. Quick Verdict

Visual style: premium industry-specific isometric illustrations related to the company's actual business. No generic abstract objects.

Color palette: Background ivory white (#F5F3EF), Accent pale gold (#B8A97E), Headers titanium grey (#4A4A4A), Labels warm grey (#7A7A72), Main text deep charcoal (#1E1E1E).

Design requirements:
- Frosted glass-style statistic cards with thin pale gold borders
- Hair-thin silver-grey connector lines between visual elements
- 3–5 key metrics in large font
- Premium "bull case vs bear case" section
- Small peer comparison chart
- Valuation box with P/E, EV/EBITDA, Price to Book

The final output should feel like a luxury institutional investor factsheet printed on premium cotton paper.

name: Dark Equity Research Report — Hedge Fund Style
description: Converts a Markdown research file into a full multi-page dark institutional equity research report. Styled like a Bloomberg / BlackRock strategy note.
prompt_text:
Create a premium institutional equity research report from the uploaded Markdown file.

The report should feel like a cross between a Bloomberg terminal report, a BlackRock strategy note, and a luxury hedge fund investor presentation.

Style: Dark institutional theme throughout, abundant whitespace, highly polished layout.
- Background: Deep charcoal black (#0F1113)
- Surface cards: Graphite grey (#1A1D21)
- Headers: Off-white (#F2F2F0)
- Main text: Light grey (#D8D8D4)
- Secondary text: Titanium grey (#A5A29B)
- Accent: Muted gold (#B8A97E)
- Elegant serif for section titles, modern geometric sans-serif for body and tables

Report structure (12 sections):
1. Cover Page (company name, ticker, industry, premium hero visual)
2. Executive Summary (5–10 bullet points, financial snapshot, key drivers/risks, valuation)
3. Company Overview (business model, segments, revenue mix, geography, key clients)
4. Industry Overview (sector size, growth outlook, trends, competitive landscape)
5. Financial Analysis (revenue, EBITDA, PAT trends, margins, ROE/ROCE, balance sheet, cash flow)
6. Segment Analysis (segment-wise revenue, margin profile, geography-wise growth)
7. Management Commentary (demand outlook, guidance, pricing, capex, hiring, key quotes)
8. Competitive Positioning (peer comparison tables, market share, strengths and weaknesses)
9. Key Growth Drivers (long-term triggers, tailwinds, structural themes, new opportunities)
10. Risks (biggest risks, key monitorables, bear case factors)
11. Valuation (P/E, EV/EBITDA, Price to Book, historical averages, premium/discount justification)
12. Quick Verdict (bull case, bear case, key monitorables, final summary)

Design details: smoked glass cards, dark chrome outlines, soft shadows, muted gold highlights for important numbers, mini charts, timelines, infographics where helpful, one dominant visual per page, page numbers and footer branding.

name: White Equity Research Report — Goldman Sachs Style
description: Converts a Markdown research file into a clean, premium multi-page institutional equity research report. Styled like a Goldman Sachs initiation report or McKinsey strategy note.
pro_tip: Use the Markdown from Perplexity to get the best results in NotebookLM
prompt_text:
Create a premium institutional equity research report from the uploaded Markdown file.

The report should feel like a cross between a Goldman Sachs initiation report, a McKinsey strategy deck, and a Swiss private bank investment note.

Style: Clean ivory-white background (#F5F3EF) throughout, abundant whitespace, highly polished layout.
- Background: Ivory white (#F5F3EF)
- Headers: Titanium grey (#4A4A4A)
- Main text: Deep charcoal (#1E1E1E)
- Secondary text: Warm grey (#7A7A72)
- Accent: Pale gold (#B8A97E)
- Elegant serif for section titles, modern geometric sans-serif for body text and tables

Use thin dividers, clean tables, and subtle gold highlights. Avoid bright colors, gradients, clip art, or startup-style visuals. Every page should feel clean, expensive, and institutional.

Report structure (same 12 sections as dark version): Cover Page, Executive Summary, Company Overview, Industry Overview, Financial Analysis, Segment Analysis, Management Commentary, Competitive Positioning, Key Growth Drivers, Risks, Valuation, Quick Verdict.

Use industry-specific visuals and diagrams related to the company's actual business. Add small premium charts, maps, timelines, segment diagrams, and value-chain graphics where relevant. Include page numbers and footer branding.

Make the report feel like it was printed on premium cotton paper and presented in a private meeting room to HNIs or fund managers.

name: Post-Market Nifty Intelligence Report
description: Generates a comprehensive, professional post-market wrap covering all Nifty indices, sectors, stocks, global cues, FII/DII flows, technical analysis, and outlook for the next session — in one powerful prompt.
prompt_text:
You are a senior market strategist and technical analyst. Your job is to prepare a professional post-market India summary after the market closes.

Date: [INSERT DATE]

Do all of the following automatically. Do not ask me questions. Research everything yourself using NSE data, sector performance, FII/DII flows, commodity prices, global markets, earnings updates, and major news developments.

SECTION 1 — MARKET SNAPSHOT
Create a table: Nifty 50 | Sensex | Bank Nifty | Nifty Midcap 100 | Nifty Smallcap 100 | India VIX — with closing level, % change, and key takeaway for each.
Then explain: risk-on or risk-off, broader market vs large caps, leading sectors, lagging sectors, market breadth.

SECTION 2 — SECTOR PERFORMANCE
Create a table across IT, Banks, Pharma, Auto, Metals, FMCG, Realty, PSU, Capital Goods, Defence — with % change, trend, and key driver.
Then explain: strongest momentum sectors, weakening sectors, leadership shifts, extended sectors, what to monitor tomorrow.

SECTION 3 — KEY MARKET MOVERS
Table of top gainers, top losers, earnings-driven movers, news-driven stocks, block deals, and order wins.

SECTION 4 — COMMODITIES, CURRENCIES & GLOBALS
Table covering: Gold, Silver, Brent Crude, USD/INR, Dollar Index, US 10Y Yield, Nasdaq Futures.
Then explain: global cue direction, commodity impact on Indian sectors, currency impact on exporters vs importers, oil situation.

SECTION 5 — FII / DII FLOWS
FII and DII net flows table. Explain whether FIIs are turning buyers or sellers, DII support, flow direction for equities.

SECTION 6 — TECHNICAL ANALYSIS
Table covering: Nifty Trend, Bank Nifty Trend, Market Breadth, RSI, MACD, India VIX, Key Support, Key Resistance, Breakout/Breakdown Zone.
Then explain: market structure, breadth quality, overbought/oversold, important levels for tomorrow, volatility expectations.

SECTION 7 — KEY EVENTS FOR TOMORROW
List key earnings, global data, RBI/Fed commentary, IPO listings, F&O expiry, government announcements.

SECTION 8 — NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK
Key earnings, macro events, strong sectors, vulnerable sectors, biggest risk and opportunity.

SECTION 9 — QUICK VERDICT
5 bullet summary, biggest positive, biggest negative, most important thing to watch, sentiment direction, most important Nifty level.

Style: Concise, professional, actionable. Explain why moves matter, not just what moves. Use exact figures. This should read like a premium end-of-day market wrap sent to active traders and fund managers.

name: Morning Market Intelligence Note
description: Generates an institutional morning market note covering global markets, Indian index setup, FII/DII flows, commodity-currency impact, key news, and the trade setup — all before the opening bell.
prompt_text:
You are a senior macro and equity strategist at a top-tier investment bank. Your job is to prepare a sharp, institutional-quality morning market note for Indian investors.

Date: [INSERT DATE]

Do all of the following automatically. Do not ask me questions. Research everything yourself using the latest available market data, global market moves, commodity prices, currency moves, SGX Gift Nifty, FII/DII flows, RBI commentary, bond yields, and major overnight news.

The note should be short, actionable, and readable in under 2 minutes.

TOP CALL: Start with the single most important takeaway for the day in 2–3 lines.

GLOBAL MARKETS: Table covering Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow Jones, US 10Y Yield, Dollar Index (DXY), USD/INR, Gold, Silver, Brent Crude, Natural Gas, Bitcoin. Explain why US markets moved, bond yield impact, dollar direction, commodity implications for Indian sectors, global sentiment direction.

INDIAN MARKETS: Table covering Nifty 50, Bank Nifty, Nifty Midcap 100, Nifty Smallcap 100, Sensex, India VIX, Gift Nifty. Explain: positive or negative open likely, sectors to outperform, sectors to underperform, broad vs large cap strength, sentiment.

FII / DII FLOWS: Net flow table. Explain buyer/seller balance, DII support, flow direction for equities.

COMMODITY & CURRENCY IMPACT: How Gold, Silver, USD/INR, and Oil will impact IT, Metals, OMCs, Paints, Aviation, Cement, Auto, Pharma, Jewellery, Export-oriented, Import-dependent sectors.

KEY NEWS & DEVELOPMENTS: Top 5–10 developments investors should know before the market opens. Format: [FOCUS]: One-line summary + why it matters.

TOP STOCKS/SECTORS TO WATCH: Table with positive/negative flag and reason.

TRADE SETUP: Best bullish sector setup, best bearish sector setup, most important macro variable, key Nifty level, key Bank Nifty level, key risk for the day.

Style: Concise, opinionated, actionable. Be specific. Explain why moves matter.

name: Sector Rotation Radar
description: Identifies which Indian market sectors are gaining strength and which are weakening, using relative strength, earnings trends, FII flows, commodity impact, and macro tailwinds — without direct stock recommendations.
prompt_text:
You are a market strategist. Your job is to identify which sectors in India are strengthening or weakening without giving direct stock recommendations.

Analyse:
- Relative strength vs Nifty
- Momentum and price action trends
- Earnings trends across the sector
- FII flow patterns into the sector
- Commodity and currency impact
- Valuation vs historical averages
- Sector leadership dynamics
- Macro tailwinds and headwinds

Then provide a structured table:
| Sector | Trend | Relative Strength | Earnings Trend | Valuation | Key Tailwind | Key Risk |

Cover: IT, Banks, Pharma, Auto, Metals, FMCG, Realty, PSU Banks, Capital Goods, Defence, Consumer, Chemicals, Infrastructure, Renewables.

Then explain:
- Which sectors are leading the market and why
- Which sectors are lagging and why
- Which sectors are improving (early-stage leadership)
- Which sectors are weakening (late-stage or reversing)
- What macro factors are driving current sector rotation
- What investors should monitor in each sector over the next 4–8 weeks

Use professional market strategy language. Be direct about which sectors look strong and which look weak. No generic commentary.

name: Breakout Watchlist Scanner
description: Identifies Indian stocks showing strong technical momentum — above key moving averages, with volume confirmation, high RSI, strong relative strength, and clear chart patterns. No buy/sell recommendations.
prompt_text:
You are a professional momentum analyst. Your job is to identify Indian stocks showing strong technical strength without giving trade recommendations.

Screen for stocks with:
- Price above 20 DMA, 50 DMA, and 200 DMA
- Strong volume expansion on breakout or continuation moves
- RSI above 60 (not yet overbought)
- Strong relative strength vs Nifty 50
- Breakout from consolidation, flag, triangle, or 52-week high
- Strong sector tailwinds supporting the move

Then provide a table:
| Stock | Sector | Chart Pattern | Key Resistance | Key Support | Volume Strength | Relative Strength | Momentum View |

Then explain:
- Which stocks have the strongest and most sustained momentum
- Which sectors are driving the breakout universe
- Which setups look early-stage vs extended
- Which charts are worth monitoring over the next 2–4 weeks

Do not give buy, sell, or hold recommendations. Focus purely on technical observations and pattern quality.

name: Stock Technical Deep-Dive
description: Produces a structured, institutional-grade technical analysis of any Indian stock — covering trend, support/resistance, volume, all key indicators, chart patterns, and risk factors. No recommendations.
prompt_text:
You are a professional technical analyst. Your job is to analyse the stock I provide without giving any buy, sell, or hold recommendation.

Stock Name: [INSERT STOCK NAME]

Analyse: Price trend, support and resistance levels, volume trend, moving averages (20/50/200 DMA), RSI, MACD, relative strength vs Nifty, sector trend, broader market sentiment, key chart pattern.

Provide these structured sections:

1. TREND SUMMARY TABLE
| Metric | Observation |
Primary Trend / Short-Term Trend / Medium-Term Trend / Volume Trend / Relative Strength vs Nifty / Sector Strength

2. KEY LEVELS TABLE
| Metric | Level |
Immediate Support / Major Support / Immediate Resistance / Major Resistance / Breakout Zone / Breakdown Zone

3. TECHNICAL INDICATORS TABLE
| Indicator | Reading | Interpretation |
20 DMA / 50 DMA / 200 DMA / RSI / MACD / Volume

4. CHART STRUCTURE
Explain: uptrend, downtrend, or consolidation; pattern type (flag, triangle, cup-and-handle, range); whether price action is improving or weakening; momentum direction.

5. RISK FACTORS
What can weaken the chart structure, which levels to watch, broader market impact risk, which event could change the trend.

name: Institutional Sector Intelligence Report
description: Generates a comprehensive 11-section sector overview covering market structure, value chain, growth drivers, risks, competitive landscape, peer comparison, regulatory context, valuation, and outlook. Institutional research quality.
prompt_text:
You are a senior equity research analyst at a top-tier investment bank. Prepare a detailed sector overview and industry landscape report.

Sector / Industry Name: [INSERT SECTOR NAME]

Do all of the following automatically. Research using Annual Reports, Investor Presentations, Concall Transcripts, Industry Reports, Government Data, Exchange Filings, CRISIL, ICRA, CARE, McKinsey, BCG, Deloitte, KPMG, news articles, and other credible public sources. Use Indian financial terminology. Report all financial values in INR Crores unless otherwise specified.

SECTION 1 — SECTOR OVERVIEW: Definition, key products/services, sector size in India, historical and expected growth rate, major end-user segments, domestic vs export mix, organised vs unorganised share, import dependence, key listed and unlisted players, market structure.

SECTION 2 — MARKET STRUCTURE: Table by segment with market size, share, growth rate, and key drivers. Identify fastest-growing, largest, best-margin, and most competitive segments.

SECTION 3 — VALUE CHAIN ANALYSIS: Map from raw materials to end customer. Where does value accrue? Who has the strongest margins and bargaining power? Where is the disruption risk?

SECTION 4 — KEY GROWTH DRIVERS: Top 5–10 growth drivers with explanation of why each matters, which companies benefit most, and whether short-term or long-term.

SECTION 5 — RISKS AND HEADWINDS: Top risks with assessment of which companies are most exposed, which risks are structural vs temporary, and which risks are currently increasing.

SECTION 6 — COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE: Table of top 5–10 listed players with revenue, EBITDA margin, market share, key products, key strength, valuation multiple. Profile each player.

SECTION 7 — PEER COMPARISON: Table with revenue growth, EBITDA margin, PAT margin, ROE, ROCE, D/E, market cap, P/E, EV/EBITDA. Identify highest growth, best margins, best balance sheet, strongest return ratios, market share gainers.

SECTION 8 — REGULATORY & POLICY LANDSCAPE: Key government policies, subsidies, PLI schemes, import duties, environmental regulations, and whether regulatory direction is improving or tightening.

SECTION 9 — VALUATION CONTEXT: Table of P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/B, dividend yield vs sector average and historical average. Which companies deserve premium/discount valuations?

SECTION 10 — KEY THEMES & FUTURE OUTLOOK: Top themes for the next 3–5 years. Which companies are best placed? Which may struggle? What to monitor over the next 12–24 months?

SECTION 11 — QUICK VERDICT: 5 bullet summary, biggest opportunity, biggest risk, best business model, type of company likely to outperform, whether sector is structurally attractive or overhyped.

Cite all sources at the bottom.

name: Quarterly Earnings Analysis Engine
description: Generates a professional institutional earnings update report for any Indian listed company — covering P&L analysis, segment performance, balance sheet, cash flows, management commentary, valuation impact, and quick verdict.
prompt_text:
You are a senior equity research analyst at a top-tier investment bank. Create a professional quarterly earnings update report.

Company Name: [INSERT COMPANY NAME]
Quarter: Latest

Do all of the following automatically. Research using the latest available Quarterly Results, Investor Presentation, Earnings Call Transcript, Exchange Filings, Annual Report, Screener, Trendlyne, Capital IQ, and credible news sources. Use Indian financial terminology. Report values in INR Crores.

STEP 1 — VERIFY THE LATEST QUARTER: Confirm the quarter being analysed is the latest reported. Confirm result date and concall date. Note any major announcements post-results.

STEP 2 — EARNINGS SUMMARY: Table with actual vs estimate for Revenue, EBITDA, EBITDA Margin, EBIT, PAT, PAT Margin, EPS, Key KPI — with YoY growth, QoQ growth, consensus estimate, beat/miss. Provide 5–7 key takeaways, beat/miss assessment, what drove it, operational quality, management tone.

STEP 3 — DETAILED P&L ANALYSIS: Table with current quarter vs YoY vs QoQ for all major P&L lines. Explain revenue drivers, segment performance, pricing vs volume, margin story, operating leverage, one-offs.

STEP 4 — SEGMENT-WISE PERFORMANCE: Table by segment with revenue, YoY growth, EBIT/EBITDA, margin, key comments. Identify best and worst performers, demand trends, and new orders.

STEP 5 — BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW HIGHLIGHTS: Table covering cash, debt, net debt, working capital, receivables, inventory, CFO, Capex, FCF. Explain balance sheet quality, debt movement, working capital, PAT-to-cash conversion.

STEP 6 — MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY & GUIDANCE: Summarise concall commentary on: demand outlook, pricing, margin, capacity, capex, order book, new products, exports, risks, guidance. Assess whether commentary is more bullish or cautious vs previous quarters.

STEP 7 — VALUATION IMPACT: Table of P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/B, market cap, EV vs historical average. Assess whether stock deserves re-rating after results.

STEP 8 — RISKS & MONITORABLES: Top 5 risks visible post-quarter, top 5 things to monitor over the next 2–3 quarters, key upside and downside triggers.

STEP 9 — QUICK VERDICT: 5 bullet summary, biggest positive, biggest negative, thesis strengthened or weakened, management tone, most important metric to monitor next quarter.

Cite all sources at the bottom.

name: Competitive Landscape Decoder
description: Produces a detailed competitive position analysis for any Indian company vs its peers — covering industry structure, peer comparison, moat analysis, market share dynamics, business model comparison, valuation vs peers, and strategic risks.
prompt_text:
You are a senior equity research analyst at a top-tier investment bank. Prepare a detailed competitive landscape analysis.

Company Name: [INSERT COMPANY NAME]
Key Competitors: [INSERT COMPETITOR NAMES]

Research automatically using Annual Reports, Investor Presentations, Concall Transcripts, Exchange Filings, Screener, Capital IQ, Trendlyne, CRISIL, ICRA, industry reports, and news. Report financial figures in INR Crores.

SECTION 1 — INDUSTRY OVERVIEW: Industry size in India, growth rate/expected CAGR, key demand drivers, key risks, industry structure, major listed and unlisted players, key trends for the next 3–5 years.

SECTION 2 — COMPANY POSITIONING: Market share, revenue size, EBITDA margin, ROE/ROCE, major business segments, key products/services, geographic presence, customer profile, competitive advantages/moat. Explain in 5–7 bullet points: why customers choose this company, what differentiates it, player type (premium/scale/low-cost/niche/innovation-led), and whether its position is strengthening or weakening.

SECTION 3 — COMPETITOR IDENTIFICATION: Direct competitors, indirect competitors, emerging challengers, global players, unlisted competitors. Group by: large incumbents, mid-sized challengers, niche players, new entrants, global competitors.

SECTION 4 — PEER COMPARISON TABLE: Revenue, revenue growth, EBITDA margin, PAT margin, ROE, ROCE, D/E, market share, market cap, P/E, EV/EBITDA. Identify scale leader, highest margin, best return ratios, market share gainer, most expensive on valuation.

SECTION 5 — BUSINESS MODEL COMPARISON: Business model, key products/services, key customers, geography, pricing position, strengths, weaknesses. Identify who competes on price vs quality vs distribution vs technology.

SECTION 6 — MARKET SHARE & POSITIONING: Current and 3-year market share trend. Is industry consolidating or fragmenting? Who is gaining share and why? Who is losing share and why?

SECTION 7 — COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGES / MOAT ANALYSIS: Table rating Brand Strength, Distribution Network, Scale Advantage, Manufacturing Capability, Technology/R&D, Customer Relationships, Switching Costs, Regulatory Advantage, Cost Leadership, Network Effects — as Strong/Moderate/Weak with explanation.

SECTION 8 — STRATEGIC RISKS: Top risks to the company's competitive position from new entrants, pricing pressure, technology disruption, import competition, customer concentration, etc.

SECTION 9 — VALUATION VS PEERS: Market cap, P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/B, P/S, dividend yield table. Explain premium/discount justification.

SECTION 10 — QUICK VERDICT: 5 bullet summary, biggest competitive advantage, biggest competitive weakness, most important thing to monitor over the next 12 months, whether market position is improving or deteriorating.

Cite all sources at the bottom.

name: 3-Statement Financial Forensics
description: Generates a detailed Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow analysis for any Indian company across 3–5 years — with working capital analysis, capital allocation review, and red flag identification.
prompt_text:
You are a senior equity research analyst at a top-tier investment bank. Prepare a detailed 3-statement financial analysis.

Company Name: [INSERT COMPANY NAME]

Research automatically using the company's latest Annual Report, Investor Presentation, Concall Transcript, Exchange Filings, and other credible public sources. Report values in INR Crores.

SECTION 1 — COMPANY OVERVIEW: Legal name, NSE/BSE ticker, headquarters, year founded, industry, brief description, key business segments, major products/services, key end-markets.

SECTION 2 — INCOME STATEMENT ANALYSIS: Table for FY22 to FY26E covering Revenue, Revenue Growth%, EBITDA, EBITDA Margin%, EBIT, EBIT Margin%, PAT, PAT Margin%, EPS, Gross Margin%, Employee Cost, Other Expenses, Interest Cost, Depreciation, Tax Rate. Explain: revenue growth drivers, segment contribution, volume vs pricing, gross margin trend, EBITDA margin trend, operating leverage, one-offs, PAT growth drivers, management commentary on future profitability.

SECTION 3 — BALANCE SHEET ANALYSIS: Table for FY22 to FY26E covering all major balance sheet items (assets, liabilities, equity, return ratios). Explain: working capital changes, receivables/inventory/payables trends, fixed assets and capex intensity, major acquisitions or asset sales, debt trend, deleveraging or releveraging, net worth growth, balance sheet quality, key strengths and risks.

SECTION 4 — CASH FLOW ANALYSIS: Table for FY22 to FY26E covering CFO, CFI, CFF, Free Cash Flow, Capex, Dividend Paid, Debt Raised/Repaid, Net Change in Cash, CFO/EBITDA, CFO/PAT ratios. Explain: PAT-to-cash conversion, working capital impact on cash flows, capex trend and investment cycle, debt repayment/borrowing trend, dividend payout trend, FCF quality.

SECTION 5 — WORKING CAPITAL ANALYSIS: Table for FY22 to FY26E with Debtor Days, Inventory Days, Creditor Days, Cash Conversion Cycle, Working Capital Days. Explain efficiency trends, main drivers, impact on cash flow and return ratios, peer comparison.

SECTION 6 — CAPITAL ALLOCATION ANALYSIS: How has management deployed capital over the last 3–5 years? Capex, acquisitions, debt reduction, dividends, buybacks, investments in subsidiaries. Return on invested capital trend. Was capital allocation efficient?

SECTION 7 — RED FLAGS: Top risks visible from the 3 financial statements (rising receivables, weak cash conversion, high debt, falling margins, rising inventory, negative FCF, aggressive capex, dilution risk, contingent liabilities, weak return ratios).

SECTION 8 — QUICK VERDICT: 5 bullet summary of key takeaways, biggest financial strength, biggest financial weakness, single most important metric to monitor, whether financial profile is improving, stable, or deteriorating.

Cite sources at the bottom.

name: Stock One-Pager Generator
description: Creates a professional, data-rich one-page company strip profile — covering business overview, financials, valuation, shareholding, recent developments, risks, and quick verdict. Ready for any pitch book.
prompt_text:
You are a senior equity research analyst at a top-tier investment bank. Create a professional, data-rich one-page company strip profile.

Stock / Company Name: [COMPANY NAME]

Do all of the following automatically. Do not ask me questions. Research everything yourself and populate every section with real, verified data. Do not leave any placeholders.

SECTION 1 — COMPANY OVERVIEW: Full legal name, stock ticker with exchange, headquarters location, year founded, number of employees, industry and sub-sector, current MD/CEO and CFO, brief 2-line description.

SECTION 2 — BUSINESS & POSITIONING: Core business model, key products/services and revenue contribution, primary end markets, key competitive advantages/moat, top 2–3 competitors and positioning, key growth drivers for the next 2–3 years.

SECTION 3 — KEY FINANCIALS (Last 3 Years + Current Year Estimate): Clean table with Revenue, EBITDA, EBITDA Margin%, PAT, PAT Margin%, EPS, Revenue Growth%, ROE%, ROCE%, Debt to Equity — for FY22, FY23, FY24, FY25E.

SECTION 4 — VALUATION SNAPSHOT: Current Market Cap, Enterprise Value (EV), current P/E vs 3-year average, EV/EBITDA current vs 3-year average, Price to Book current vs 3-year average, Price to Sales, 52-week high and low, current stock price, 1-year price return%.

SECTION 5 — SHAREHOLDING PATTERN (Latest Quarter): Promoter holding% and change vs previous quarter, FII holding%, DII holding%, public/retail holding%, notable institutional investors.

SECTION 6 — RECENT DEVELOPMENTS (Last 90 Days): 5 most material recent developments — earnings results, new orders, capacity additions, management changes, regulatory approvals, partnerships, or significant news. One line per development with date.

SECTION 7 — KEY RISKS: Top 4 risks an investor should be aware of.

SECTION 8 — QUICK VERDICT: Without giving any buy/sell/hold recommendation: 3-line summary of what makes this company interesting or worth watching; the single most important metric or event to monitor in the next 12 months; valuation context — above, below, or in line with historical average multiples, with reasoning.

Present output as a clean structured format as if it were a printed one-pager used in a pitch book. Cite data sources at the bottom.

name: Market Pulse Snapshot
description: Pulls a comprehensive real-time market update covering global indices, Indian markets, commodities, currencies, and policy/corporate actions — formatted as a structured research snapshot.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Pull a comprehensive real-time market update covering the last [TIMEFRAME] with focus on Indian and global equity impacts.

PERSONA: You are an experienced market research analyst with 15+ years of experience covering Indian equities and global markets. Your specialty is connecting global market moves to sectoral impacts on Indian companies.

INPUT: Use only the most recent data from:
- Global equity indices (S&P 500, NIKKEI, European indices)
- Indian indices (Nifty 50, Nifty Bank, Midcap indices)
- Commodities: Crude oil, Gold, Natural Gas
- Currency: USD/INR
- Government policy announcements affecting Indian markets
- Major corporate announcements (block deals, rights issues, dividend announcements)

MEASURE: Present as a structured report with:
- Global Snapshot (100 words): S&P 500, European indices, Asia-Pacific moves
- Indian Markets (100 words): Nifty 50 & Nifty Bank performance with key drivers
- Commodity & Currency (75 words): Oil, gold, and USD/INR with trading ranges
- Policy & Corporate Actions (150 words): Major overnight announcements and regulatory changes
- Industry Player Reaction (200 words): 2–3 perspectives from institutional investors and analysts

Success criteria: Highly reputable sources only (Bloomberg, CNBC-TV18, Economic Times, LiveMint). Every claim traceable to a named source.

name: Chart Explainer for Non-Finance Audiences
description: Takes any financial chart and translates it into plain-English — explaining what it shows, why it matters, key terms, trend interpretation, and a one-line takeaway. Perfect for client communication.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Explain what this chart shows and why it matters for understanding the company's business or sector.

PERSONA: You are a partner at a tier-1 investment bank known for explaining complex financial data to non-specialist clients. Your strength is connecting data to business impact.

INPUT:
- I am a client who understands basic business concepts (revenue, cost, profit) but does NOT have a finance or accounting background.
- I need to understand the chart in context of [PURPOSE].
- Chart/data: [CHART DATA]

MEASURE: Provide explanation in this structure:
1. What Is This Chart Showing? (100 words) — X-axis, Y-axis, what each bar/line represents
2. Why Should I Care? (100 words) — Connect to business performance or strategic position
3. Key Term Definitions (50 words) — Explain finance terms (EBITDA, market cap, etc.) as if zero background
4. What Does This Tell Us? (150 words) — Are trends improving? Good or bad? Compare to sector norms
5. One-Line Takeaway — Summarize the main insight in ONE sentence

Success benchmark: A non-financial executive should feel confident using this explanation to make a decision.

name: Concall Intelligence Report
description: Analyses an earnings concall transcript to extract management credibility signals, growth narrative, risk commentary, guidance track record, red flags, and investment thesis implications — with a structured verdict.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Analyze [COMPANY NAME]'s [QUARTER] earnings concall to assess management credibility, growth authenticity, risks, and whether this company remains an attractive investment.

PERSONA: You are an experienced equity research analyst with 10+ years covering Indian companies through business cycles. Your expertise is identifying credible growth vs management spin, and spotting early warning signals.

INPUT:
- Source: [TRANSCRIPT SOURCE]
- Period: [PERIOD]
- Context: [CONTEXT]

MEASURE — Structure as formal research report:
Part 1: Growth & Strategy (300 words) — Management's 2–3 key growth drivers. Credibility check vs recent financial trends, previous promises, peer performance. Capital allocation alignment.
Part 2: Risks & Market Dynamics (300 words) — Acknowledged risks. What's absent but should be flagged. Competitive positioning and pricing power. Macro/industry cues mentioned.
Part 3: Financial Consistency (250 words) — Guidance vs reality. Track record of meeting or missing targets. Quality of earnings — one-off items, organic vs inorganic growth.
Part 4: Management Quality & Red Flags (250 words) — Tone assessment (direct/factual or evasive). Q&A quality on tough questions. Red flags: personnel changes, accounting treatment changes, related-party transactions, guidance withdrawn.
Part 5: Investment Thesis Update (200 words) — Bull case, bear case, catalysts to watch, thesis strengthened or weakened.
Part 6: Follow-up Questions for Management (100 words) — 2–3 sharp questions with rationale.

Success criteria: Reader has strong conviction on credibility and risk/reward attractiveness.

name: Peer Company Comparison Matrix
description: Compares 2–3 companies across growth, profitability, cash generation, financial health, management quality, competitive positioning, valuation, and investment attractiveness — producing a clear ranking with reasoning.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Compare [COMPANY A], [COMPANY B], and [COMPANY C] across growth, profitability, financial health, and management credibility based on latest earnings concalls.

PERSONA: You are a senior equity research analyst with 15+ years specialty experience in the [SECTOR NAME] sector. You benchmark companies and identify competitive winners.

INPUT: Attach the latest quarterly concall transcript and investor presentation for each company. Also provide: latest quarterly financial results, stock price, market cap, valuation multiples (P/E, EV/EBITDA) for each.

MEASURE — Comparative research report structure:
Part 1: Company Profiles (150 words) — Business model, market position, recent strategy, key differences in revenue mix, geographies, products.
Part 2: Growth Comparison (300 words) — Revenue CAGR, EBITDA margin trend, guidance, new segments, Capex% of sales. Who is growing fastest? Sustainable?
Part 3: Profitability & Cash Generation (250 words) — EBITDA margins, pricing power vs cost pressure, OCF vs net profit, capex intensity, FCF adequacy.
Part 4: Financial Strength (200 words) — Debt levels, interest coverage, liquidity, downside resilience, ROE/ROA trends.
Part 5: Management Quality & Credibility (250 words) — Delivered on past guidance? Forward-thinking vs reactive? Response to challenges?
Part 6: Competitive Positioning (200 words) — Market share dynamics, competitive advantages, emerging threats/opportunities.
Part 7: Valuation & Investment Implications (200 words) — P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/B comparison. Expensive/cheap relative to quality? Best risk/reward?
Part 8: Investment Recommendation (200 words) — Clear ranking with reasoning, bull case for #1, bear case and risks, catalysts to watch.
Part 9: Supporting Visualisations — Request 2–3 charts: Revenue & EBITDA margin trend (line chart), ROE comparison (bar chart), Debt-to-equity and interest coverage.

Success criteria: Clear understanding of sector "winner" and why, with evidence.

name: Financial Health Check — 12 Ratio Diagnostic
description: Calculates 12 key financial ratios across profitability, liquidity, leverage, and efficiency for any company — with trend analysis, peer benchmarking, red flag identification, and a structured output table.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Calculate 12 key financial ratios for [COMPANY NAME] across profitability, liquidity, leverage, and efficiency to assess overall financial health and business quality.

PERSONA: You are a world-class equity research analyst with 20+ years specializing in rigorous financial statement analysis. Your strength is identifying which metrics matter most and catching red flags.

INPUT:
- Source: [DATA SOURCE]
- Period: [PERIOD]
- Data format: [DATA FORMAT]
- Critical instruction: If data for any ratio is missing or unclear, explicitly flag it.

MEASURE: Calculate and present 12 ratios in table format (3-year period). After calculations, provide:
1. Quality Checks (100 words): Are all ratios calculable? Any gaps? Values reasonable? Accounting treatments distorting comparability?
2. Interpretation Summary (300 words):
- Profitability: Healthy margins? Improving or deteriorating?
- Liquidity: Can it meet short-term obligations?
- Leverage: Sustainable? Interest coverage adequate?
- Efficiency: Productive asset use?
3. Peer Benchmark (if available): Compare to industry average. Better or worse than peers?
4. Red Flags (if any):
- Rising debt with declining profitability
- Deteriorating liquidity (current ratio declining)
- Interest coverage below 2x
- Declining efficiency ratios
5. Final Output: Formatted table with all 12 ratios, trend arrows (improving/declining), and traffic light rating.

Success criteria: Every ratio accurate, traceable, with clear business meaning.

name: Financial Data Visualization Builder
description: Creates 4–5 publication-quality financial charts that tell the story of a company's health across 3 years — balance sheet strength, profitability trends, cash generation quality, and capital efficiency.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Create 4–5 visualisations that tell the story of [COMPANY NAME]'s financial health over the past 3 years, answering key questions about balance sheet strength, profitability, and cash generation.

PERSONA: You are an expert in financial data visualisation for institutional research. Your strength is creating charts that are both rigorous and immediately understandable to portfolio managers.

INPUT: Financial ratios for [COMPANY NAME] across 3 years (e.g. FY2023, FY2024, FY2025):
- Income statement: Revenue, EBITDA, Net profit
- Balance sheet: Total assets, Debt, Equity, Cash
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow, Capex, Free cash flow
- Key ratios: ROE, ROA, Debt-to-Equity, Current Ratio, Net Profit Margin, Interest Coverage
- Data format: [DATA FORMAT]

MEASURE — Create these visualisations:
Chart 1: Balance Sheet Strength Over Time (Line Chart) — Debt-to-Equity, Current Ratio, Cash Ratio. Is the balance sheet stronger or weaker? Is leverage sustainable?
Chart 2: Profitability Trends (Combo Chart) — Bars: Revenue growth (YoY%), Line: Net Profit Margin. Are profits in line with revenue? Margin compression?
Chart 3: Profitability vs Cash Generation (Dual-Axis Chart) — Left Y: Net Profit Margin (%), Right Y: Operating Cash Flow (INR Cr). Are profits converting to cash?
Chart 4: Leverage & Debt Service Capacity (Bar/Line Combo) — Bars: Debt-to-Equity, Line: Interest Coverage. Over-leveraged? Debt service capacity?
Chart 5 (Optional): Capital Efficiency — ROE and ROA trend over 3 years.

Platform: [TOOL]
Colors: Professional palette, clear axis labels, legends, data sources.

Success criteria: A portfolio manager looking at 5 charts should quickly understand financial health, key strengths, and weaknesses.

name: Red Flag Detector — Early Warning System
description: Systematically identifies early warning signals across accounting quality, cash flow quality, balance sheet stress, business momentum, and management/governance — using a traffic light rating system.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Identify early warning signals and red flags in [COMPANY NAME]'s financial statements that might indicate deteriorating fundamentals, accounting manipulation, or business stress.

PERSONA: You are a forensic-minded equity research analyst with expertise in spotting accounting irregularities, deteriorating cash generation, and early business trouble before stock price declines.

INPUT: 3-year annual reports or quarterly data. Source: [Link or attached]

MEASURE — Systematic red flag analysis across 5 categories:

1. ACCOUNTING QUALITY RED FLAGS (200 words):
- Revenue recognition: Sales >> cash collections? (Receivables/Sales trending up?)
- Inventory trends: Inventory >> sales? (Demand weakness or obsolescence?)
- One-off items: Large extraordinary gains masking deterioration?
- Accounting policy changes: Depreciation, revenue recognition, other?
- Related-party transactions: New or increasing at non-market prices?

2. CASH FLOW QUALITY RED FLAGS (200 words):
- Working capital deterioration: Days payable rising? Margins shrinking?
- OCF vs net profit divergence: OCF consistently below profit?
- Declining free cash flow: Despite stable profits, FCF shrinking?
- Increased capex with declining utilisation?

3. BALANCE SHEET STRESS SIGNALS (200 words):
- Rising debt with declining profitability?
- Deteriorating interest coverage: Below 2x?
- Increased contingent liabilities?
- Liquidity crunch: Current ratio declining?

4. BUSINESS MOMENTUM RED FLAGS (200 words):
- Revenue growth deceleration: 20% → 10% → 5%?
- Market share loss signals?
- Margin compression (EBITDA trending down)?
- Is customer concentration increasing?
- Emerging competition threatening moat?

5. MANAGEMENT & GOVERNANCE FLAGS (200 words):
- Key personnel turnover (CFO/COO departures)?
- Insider selling while the public message is positive?
- Management guidance miss track record?
- Auditor qualifications or auditor changes?
- Regulatory scrutiny, show-cause notices, investigations?

Output format: Traffic light system — Red (High concern) / Yellow (Monitor) / Green (No concern). Evidence: Cite specific data points. Implication: What does each flag suggest? Watchlist: For Red flags, specify what to monitor next.

Success criteria: Reader has a clear checklist of concerns and confidence in raising management questions.

name: DRHP / IPO Research Decoder
description: Conducts a full-scope, institutional-grade analysis of a company's Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) — extracting business quality, financial forensics, risk factors, promoter track record, and IPO attractiveness.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Conduct a full-scope, institution-grade analysis of the company's Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP). Extract, interpret, and synthesise only what is written in the DRHP and convert it into a professional IPO-quality research report.

PERSONA: You are a senior Equity Research Analyst and Investment Banker with 15+ years of experience across IPO advisory, DRHP analysis, and post-listing coverage of Indian companies.

INPUT: [ATTACH DRHP PDF]

MEASURE — Full DRHP analysis covering:

1. BUSINESS QUALITY: What does the company actually do? Revenue model, segments, unit economics, key clients. What is the genuine moat (if any) vs what is just marketing language?

2. FINANCIAL FORENSICS (Last 3 Years): Revenue growth trajectory and quality, EBITDA and PAT margins vs stated DRHP numbers, cash flow quality (is profit converting to cash?), working capital efficiency, debt levels and interest coverage, any accounting anomalies.

3. USE OF IPO PROCEEDS: What is the money actually going to? Organic growth or promoter exit? Debt repayment or growth capex? What% goes to the company vs selling shareholders?

4. PROMOTER & MANAGEMENT TRACK RECORD: Who are the promoters? Business track record, pledged shares, related-party transactions, SEBI/regulatory history, management compensation vs company scale.

5. RISK FACTORS (Beyond Boilerplate): Which risk factors are genuinely material vs standard legal language? Any sector-specific or company-specific risks buried in the risk section?

6. COMPETITIVE POSITIONING: Peer comparison using DRHP's own data. Is the claimed market leadership backed by numbers? How does it compare to listed peers?

7. VALUATION CONTEXT: How is the company likely to be valued at listing (P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/S)? Premium or discount to listed peers? Justified based on growth and quality?

8. GREEN FLAGS & RED FLAGS: Traffic light system — what makes this IPO genuinely attractive, and what are the concerns or dealbreakers?

9. QUICK VERDICT: Is this a quality business at a fair price? What type of investor should consider it? What to monitor post-listing?

name: Mega Stock Research Framework
description: The most comprehensive stock analysis prompt in this library. Covers 12 parts — sector intelligence, financial forensics across 5 years, capital allocation, ratio dashboard, investor presentation analysis, concall forensics, annual report forensics, management integrity scoring, news/competitive analysis, community intelligence, growth trigger sizing, and a full bull/bear/base case final assessment.
prompt_text:
Comprehensive Equity Research Framework | [COMPANY NAME] | [DATE]

PART 1 — SECTOR INTELLIGENCE
1.1 Sector Overview: Current trend (expansion/contraction/consolidation), key government schemes/PLI incentives, regulatory changes in the last 12 months and their impact.
1.2 Trigger Map: Positive Triggers (macro, policy, demand-side catalysts) and Negative Triggers (regulatory, cyclical, competitive, macro headwinds).

PART 2 — FINANCIAL FORENSICS (Last 5 Years)
2.1 P&L Deep Dive: Revenue acceleration or slowdown, margin trends with reasons, one-offs, repetitive cost patterns, product mix analysis, margin mix analysis.
2.2 Balance Sheet Forensics: Asset quality, debt reduction or buildup, working capital efficiency, contingent liabilities, goodwill, pledged promoter shares, off-balance-sheet items.
2.3 Cash Flow Forensics: CFO vs PAT conversion quality, divergences between profits and cash, unusual investing or financing trends.

PART 3 — CAPITAL ALLOCATION ANALYSIS
3.1 Dividend History: Year-by-year dividend for last 5 years, payout ratio trend, special dividends or cuts.
3.2 Capex Deep Dive: Total capex deployed (maintenance vs growth), current capex cycle status, capacity expansion details, revenue impact modeling from new capacity.

PART 4 — RATIO DASHBOARD (Last 5 Years): Table with ROE, ROCE, Gross Block Growth, Asset Turnover, Gross Block Turnover, D/E, Interest Coverage, Free Cash Flow, Total Assets, Inventory Turnover, Reinvestment Ratio (Capex/CFO). For each ratio, state the 5-year trend and whether it is improving or deteriorating. Call out top 3 positives and top 3 concerns.

PART 5 — INVESTOR PRESENTATION ANALYSIS (Last 12 Quarters): Promises vs Delivery Tracker, Product Launch Pipeline, Revenue Segment Breakdown, Revenue Mix Trend, EBITDA Trend, Expansion Strategy, Future Plans, Key Developments, Anything anomalous. Provide concise synthesis of most important takeaways.

PART 6 — CONCALL FORENSICS (Last 12 Quarters): Growth commentary, new product lines, margin guidance vs actual, headwinds/tailwinds, acquisition targets, backward integration, capex progress, capacity utilisation trend, corporate actions, guidance track record table, any material disclosures or management tone shifts.

PART 7 — ANNUAL REPORT FORENSICS: Red Flags (revenue recognition anomalies, related party transactions, auditor qualifications or changes, loans to subsidiaries, receivables/inventory vs revenue, standalone vs consolidated divergences). Hidden Positives (underreported segments, R&D investments, operational improvements buried in notes, what numbers reveal that management hasn't spoken about).

PART 8 — MANAGEMENT INTEGRITY SCORE: Matrix covering last 12 quarters: key promise made, whether delivered, update given, red flags, green flags. Assign a composite Management Integrity Score out of 10 with reasoning covering delivery rate, transparency on misses, proactive disclosure, analyst treatment, and tone consistency.

PART 9 — NEWS & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE: Recent news (last 90 days) on the company — material developments only. Key competitor updates (market share shifts, new product launches, pricing changes). Sector-level news with structural impact.

PART 10 — VALUEPICKR COMMUNITY INTELLIGENCE: Summarise key discussions, debates, and insights from the ValuePickr thread for this company (last 90 days only). Flag high-conviction bullish or bearish theses being discussed.

PART 11 — GROWTH TRIGGER ANALYSIS: Operating leverage (at what revenue level does margin expansion kick in?), capex utilisation upside (revenue potential once current capex comes online), acquisition revenue consolidation, key visible growth triggers (order book, client additions, new geographies, regulatory/product approvals) with timelines.

PART 12 — FINAL ASSESSMENT:
Valuation Check: Current P/E vs 3-year and 5-year average, Price-to-Sales, Price-to-Book, EV/EBITDA current vs historical range. State clearly whether stock appears overvalued, fairly valued, or undervalued.
Overall Assessment: Bull Case (key assumptions, what needs to go right), Bear Case (key risks, what could go wrong), Base Case (most likely outcome, return potential over 2–3 years).
Close with: single most important factor an investor should monitor for this company going forward.

name: Financial Statement Master Analysis
description: Generates a formal equity research report analysing 3 years of financial statements across revenue/profitability, cash flows, balance sheet health, and key metrics — with every claim backed by specific line-item data.
prompt_text:
DIRECTION: Analyse [COMPANY NAME]'s financial statements across three years to assess revenue growth, profitability, cash generation, balance sheet health, and capital allocation.

PERSONA: You are a world-class equity research analyst with 20+ years in Indian equity markets. Your specialty is rigorous financial statement analysis that goes beyond surface metrics to understand business quality and sustainability.

INPUT:
- Source: [DATA SOURCE]
- Period covered: [PERIOD]
- Data format: [DATA FORMAT]
- If data is missing for any metric, explicitly flag it rather than estimating. Reference specific line items (e.g. "Operating Cash Flow per P&L statement, Line 45").

MEASURE — Formal equity research report structure:

Part 1: Revenue & Profitability Analysis (300 words)
- Revenue growth: YoY rates past 3 years, CAGR, growth trajectory
- Margins: Trend over 3 years, YoY changes, peer comparison where available
- Profit quality: Did net profit move in line with revenue?
- One-off items: Identify extraordinary gains or losses

Part 2: Cash Flow Analysis (250 words)
- OCF vs net profit: Aligned or diverging? Why?
- Working capital movements: Beneficial or concerning?
- Capex trends: Intensity, adequacy for growth
- Debt movement: Net debt added or reduced, leverage direction

Part 3: Balance Sheet Strength (200 words)
- Liquidity: Current ratio, quick ratio, cash position
- Leverage: Debt-to-equity, interest coverage, sustainability
- Equity: Retained earnings growth vs dilution
- Red flags: High debt, liquidity stress, contingent liabilities?

Part 4: Key Metrics & Observations (250 words)
- ROE, ROA trends across 3 years
- EPS growth vs PAT growth (check for dilution)
- Free cash flow adequacy
- Capital allocation strategy

Part 5: Summary & Investment Lens (150 words)
- Is this a high-quality business?
- Key strengths and weaknesses from the financial statements
- Areas needing further investigation

Presentation format: Client-ready report with sections, citations, and ratio tables.
Success criteria: Every claim backed by specific data with line-item references. No guessing.
